danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental hazards scattered across playing surface
danger_signals.meaning: Instant threat to player survival; loss of health or death during play

danger_signals.visual_form: Open flames visible on field boundaries and interior
danger_signals.meaning: Hazardous terrain; players can be killed by contact or pushed into flames

danger_signals.visual_form: Field composed of toxic waste material instead of standard grass
danger_signals.meaning: Extreme environmental danger; cumulative damage to players

danger_signals.visual_form: Field boundaries open to empty space instead of stands
danger_signals.meaning: Lethal fall hazard; players can be knocked off into void

danger_signals.visual_form: Player sprite removal or death state during play
danger_signals.meaning: Permanent loss of team member; contributes to forfeit condition
Unexpected lethal play event; designed to kill opposing players

safety_signals.visual_form: Rock, ice, or rubber material (non-toxic variants)
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced environmental danger; lower baseline hazard than toxic/flame fields

safety_signals.visual_form: Ref sprite on field during play
safety_signals.meaning: Rules enforcement active; some protection from illegal play (though bribing corrupts this)

safety_signals.visual_form: Active play state with time progression
safety_signals.meaning: Structured engagement; safety within bounded time frame

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Team-specific uniform/jersey visuals (inferred from real-world team analogues)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opposing team displays distinct visual palette and player type composition (aliens vs. skeletons vs. robots, etc.)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Team-themed stadium hazard arrangement (e.g., Deathskin Razors field vs. Midway Monsters field)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinguishable from skeleton, robot, troll, and superhuman archetypes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Teams composed of aliens occupy distinct visual team slots

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinguishable from alien, robot, troll, and superhuman archetypes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Undead aesthetic; associated with death/post-apocalyptic setting

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinguishable from alien, skeleton, troll, and superhuman archetypes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Technology-based aesthetic; associated with industrial hazards

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinguishable from alien, skeleton, robot, and superhuman archetypes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Creature-based aesthetic; associated with primal violence

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinguishable from alien, skeleton, robot, and troll archetypes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Field hazards (mines, fire pits, toxic waste, void edges); non-standard field materials (ice, rubber, toxic waste instead of grass); dead rising from graves (skeleton players); radiation-induced mutations visible in player morphologies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Violence normalized as sport; rules corruption (bribe ref mechanic); killing for strategic advantage (forfeit by attrition); MVP punishment (induction into Hole of Flame)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien player type present on multiple teams; alien morphology visible on field
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aliens participate as organized team members; coexist with other mutant types in unified team structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Referee can be killed mid-game; replacement refs appear progressively tougher; phony penalty calls visible in scoreboard/UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bribe ref mechanic allows temporary rule inversion; teams can murder referee; murdered ref replaced by tougher enforcement; no penalty for accidental ref deaths (system acknowledges ref vulnerability)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Losing team explodes spontaneously (death animation); winning MVP seized by referees
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Winner receives ceremonial punishment (Hole of Flame induction); losing team permanent elimination; visual destruction of defeated faction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player count on field decreases visually as members are killed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dead players cause fumbles during play; seven-player limit means each death reduces offensive/defensive capability; forfeit triggered when enough players eliminated

environmental_storytelling.technique: Field hazard placement as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Landmines, fire pits, toxic waste fields, and void edges communicate that the world is actively hostile and unsuitable for normal human civilization; hazards are permanent world features, not removable obstacles

environmental_storytelling.technique: Field material composition as thematic storytelling
environmental_storytelling.example: Rock, ice, rubber, and toxic waste field variants suggest different post-apocalyptic ecosystems; toxic waste fields specifically convey radiation damage still active in environment

Skeleton players (the dead risen); alien players (invasion occurred); robot players (technology persists); troll players (mutation created new species); superhuman players (selective mutation benefits) — all visible together communicate layered catastrophe history

environmental_storytelling.technique: Victory ceremony as cultural degradation signal
environmental_storytelling.example: MVP punishment (Hole of Flame induction) rather than celebration shows civilization has inverted values; winners receive ritual sacrifice; losing team explosion suggests total faction elimination as acceptable outcome

environmental_storytelling.technique: Referee killability as authority collapse signal
environmental_storytelling.example: Refs can be murdered with no consequence (Ref Bashing penalty applied to murderers, then they face replacement); communicates that institutional authority is physically vulnerable and can be eliminated through violence
Ability to bribe ref twice per game with resulting phony penalties (e.g., '5-yard penalty for crying') signals that formal rules are suspended and violence/resource expenditure supersedes law

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Normal movement, blocking, tackling, passing; contributes to team offensive/defensive capability
Fumbles during play; reduced performance; vulnerability to being killed
Removed from field; permanent loss; team is one player shorter
Standard penalty enforcement (offsides, QB bashing, pass interference, delay of game calls); maintains rule structure
Calls phony penalties on opposing team (arbitrary penalties like 'crying'); lasts for several plays; opposing team typically attempts to kill ref during this state
Replaced by tougher referee model; replacement immediately calls 5-yard Ref Bashing penalty on team that murdered predecessor
MVP is seized and inducted into Hole of Flame (death by fire); team survives but leader is ritually sacrificed
Entire team explodes spontaneously (visual death animation); total faction elimination
Forced to forfeit game; victory awarded to surviving team through attrition rather than score
Executes special violent trick (exploding ball, invisibility, electric shock, jet pack, QB assassination); high-risk high-reward play designed to kill or incapacitate opposing players

notes: Mutant League Football communicates world-state through comprehensive visual and mechanical language of violence normalization. The post-apocalyptic setting is not merely aesthetic but mechanically embedded: hazardous fields are permanent, player death is permanent, authority is killable, and victory requires survival through attrition. The game's faction system uses morphological diversity (aliens, skeletons, robots, trolls, superhumans) to visually encode the layered nature of the catastrophe (invasion + radiation mutation + undead resurrection + technology persistence). Environmental storytelling relies on hazard placement and field material composition to reinforce unsuitability for civilized life. The most significant danger signal is the normalization of lethal play as sports strategy; the most significant world-state signal is the inversion of victory ceremony into ritual sacrifice (MVP Hole of Flame induction). Authority is communicated as fragile and corruptible through the bribe mechanic and referee killability. No factions are explicitly allied or opposed; instead, the game communicates a universal post-apocalyptic condition where all player types coexist in violent competition regardless of origin.
